title: The Dry Peak Silence

features.name: peak burl
features.desc-on-touch: A living node hardware casing reaching the mountain's top. The antenna here receives nothing, broadcasts to no one. The archive listens at this height and hears only what the ridge remembers: six voices singing a harmony that stopped.

features.name: silence pool
features.desc-on-touch: Where the rivers don't reach, where all the six voices have fallen silent. A pool that holds only the shape of sound—you can see the ripples of harmony that ended, the pressure of voices that stopped mid-note.

features.name: fence-post glyph row
features.desc-on-touch: Each post a marker, each mark a moment the rivers learned to sing together. Now they are silent. The posts stand in ordered rows, counting the silence in regular intervals.

features.name: listening tower
features.desc-on-touch: A stack of listening-stones reaching toward sky. Each layer is tuned to a different silence. The archive climbs and learns that after six voices sing, the seventh voice is the land refusing to join.

inscription: checksum: inaudibly valid / ecologically unstable—the silence exceeds all previous listening thresholds; the archive has learned that not-hearing is also a form of record

file.name: The_Convergence_Ridge_Harmonic_Cessation_Recorded.mp3
file.note: An audio document that contains only silence. The spectrogram shows the ghost of six-part harmony. The archive cannot decide if the harmony is stored in the silence or if the silence is all that remai

exits.label: back down the ridge toward the rivers that still remember singing
further into the high country where silence becomes landscape
exits.to: NEW: the peak where nothing grows and listening becomes a solitary act

exits.label: down-ridge, toward the understory gathering where voices wait
